skill.name: pitfalls-blockchain
skill.description: Guidance for reviewing blockchain RPC calls, gas estimation, multicall handling, nonce management, and multi-chain transaction configuration.
skill.summary: Review common blockchain transaction pitfalls before shipping smart contract integrations.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment, wallet, weak crypto, and combined-risk patterns. Review found these are Markdown and TypeScript examples, not executable skill code, with no evidence of prompt injection or malicious intent. The only residual concerns are documentation examples that mention RPC endpoints and environment variables.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are fenced Markdown code blocks or a TypeScript template string. No executable skill script, shell command, or instruction to run commands is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visibly part of Markdown fences or illustrative TypeScript. I found no file content that executes shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded RPC URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are example fallback RPC endpoints in a code sample. They do not transmit data by themselves, but users should replace placeholder keys with their own configured providers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are inside a documented TypeScript array named RPC_ENDPOINTS. There is no active network call or exfiltration logic in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable References Are Non-Secret RPC Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: The environment variable references are example RPC URL configuration fields. No private key, wallet seed, environment file read, or outbound transmission of secret values is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment variables shown are ETHEREUM_RPC_URL and POLYGON_RPC_URL in a configuration example. I found no evidence of .env file access or wallet credential handling.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Wallet And Weak Crypto Findings Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The blockchain domain text mentions chain configuration and execution reverts, which triggered sensitive and weak-crypto rules. No wallet private key, seed phrase, cryptographic implementation, or weak hash algorithm is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found domain terminology and RPC configuration only. I did not find cryptographic code or wallet material in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Combined Critical Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical combination of code execution, network, and credential access is not supported by the file contents. The apparent signals come from Markdown examples, placeholder RPC URLs, and non-secret RPC environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All suspicious indicators resolve to static examples in one Markdown file. I found no executable payload, prompt injection text, or data exfiltration path.

content.value_statement: Blockchain integrations often fail because RPC calls, gas estimates, and nonces are handled too casually. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a checklist for safer smart contract transaction reviews.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether contract calls include try/catch error handling.
Reviews gas estimation, gas buffers, and EIP-1559 fee handling.
Highlights safer multicall behavior with per-call failure handling.
Guides multi-chain RPC configuration and confirmation settings.
Reviews nonce management patterns for concurrent transactions.
Provides a concise checklist for blockchain code review.
limitations: Does not execute transactions or connect to blockchain networks.
Does not verify deployed contract bytecode or on-chain state.
Does not replace a smart contract security audit.
Uses general guidance and may need adaptation for each chain.

use_cases.title: Review DApp Transaction Code
use_cases.description: Find missing error handling, unsafe gas usage, and weak retry behavior before a frontend sends transactions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Multi-Chain Integrations
use_cases.description: Check RPC configuration, confirmations, native tokens, and fallback behavior across supported chains.

use_cases.title: Audit Transaction Reliability
use_cases.description: Use a practical checklist to review nonce handling, simulations, and revert handling in transaction services.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic RPC Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a contract call
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this blockchain RPC call for common pitfalls. Focus on try/catch handling, revert behavior, and safe fallback behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review Gas Estimation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this transaction code for gas estimation issues. Check buffers, EIP-1559 fee fields, simulation before send, and gas spike handling.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Multi-Chain Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this multi-chain configuration. Check RPC URL handling, chain IDs, confirmation counts, native token names, and fallback endpoint strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Perform Full Transaction Reliability Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full reliability review of this blockchain transaction flow. Cover RPC failures, multicall safety, gas estimation, nonce management, retries, simulations, and confirmations.

output_examples.input: Review a swap transaction flow that estimates gas and submits a contract call.
output: The review flags missing simulation before transaction submission.
It recommends adding a gas buffer after estimation.
It notes that revert handling should return a clear user-facing failure state.

output_examples.input: Review a multi-chain configuration for Ethereum and Polygon.
output: The review checks that each chain has its own RPC URL and confirmation count.
It warns against hardcoding provider keys in source files.
It recommends documenting fallback endpoint behavior.

output_examples.input: Review a service that sends several transactions for the same wallet.
output: The review identifies nonce collision risk during concurrent sends.
It recommends tracking pending nonces against the provider state.
It suggests waiting for enough confirmations before marking work complete.

best_practices: Use the checklist before sending or reviewing transaction code.
Provide the relevant chain, provider, and contract context in the prompt.
Validate recommendations against current provider and wallet behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not treat example RPC endpoints as production configuration.
Do not skip transaction simulation for state-changing calls.
Do not use one nonce source for concurrent transaction workers without coordination.

faq.question: Does this skill execute blockchain transactions?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and examples only.

faq.question: Can it replace a smart contract audit?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on integration pitfalls, not formal contract security.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require private keys?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not request or use private keys.

faq.question: What blockchain topics does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers RPC errors, gas estimation, multicall safety, nonce management, retries, and confirmations.

faq.question: Should teams adapt the examples?
faq.answer: Yes. Chain behavior, provider limits, and confirmation policies vary by application.
